text.tspan: Total Size: 8,256 Bytes (M=8 subspaces, K=256 centroids)

g.title: Memory-aligned float32 array used for Asymmetric Distance Computation.

div.p: Given a quantized vector with codes: [i₀, i₁, ..., i₇]

ol.li:
FOR m FROM 0 TO 7:
Row Start = m * 1024 (where 1024 = 256 * 4)
Entry Offset = codes[m] * 4
Sum += table[Row Start + Entry Offset]

div.p: Complexity: O(M) lookups. This avoids computing full-dimensional floating point distances at query time.

g.title: Centroid distances for the first quantization subspace.
Centroid distances for the second quantization subspace.
